what_it_does: Implements the Streams workspace and reusable StreamRunner/StreamLauncher/StreamHost React components that execute MS_STREAMS guided sequences step-by-step. It renders per-step bodies (gather, preference, decision, options, produce, review, synthesize, ask) and commits real changes through global registries like MS_CONTEXT, MS_MEMORY, MS_DECISIONS, MS_ARTIFACTS, and MS_PRODUCE.
observations: The same StreamRunner is used for initial project setup and in-project sub-sequences.
Per-step bodies are pedagogical: each step names its MOVE and explains what it does.
Step rendering delegates to global registry components and helpers (QuestionBody, CandidateCard, StepDirection, Deductions, ViLearned, FeedbackLayer).
Live commit handlers mutate project state: gather adds sources, decide applies tweaks, produce creates artifacts, review sets status/memory.
An understanding/gating bar is shown for non-synthesize steps.
The synthesize phase reuses the old wizard SHAPE steps (direction, renderer, sequence, ready) and can show a Deductions panel in fullscreen.
Project-init streams mirror the assembled brief into MS_CONTEXT via a useEffect keyed by a serialized answer signature.
StreamHost is an embeddable launcher+overlay runner that any page can drop in.
Class names use a strm- prefix and reuse dsys/comp/genopt classes.
The file relies almost entirely on window.* globals rather than imported modules.
standouts: A single StreamRunner unifies project initialization and in-project guided sub-sequences.
STSynthesize bridges the new stream mechanism to the existing wizard SHAPE phase components.
Commit handlers turn each step into a real mutating operation across multiple global registries.
StreamHost makes guided streams available as an overlay on any page.
conventions: Functional React components with hooks aliased as useStateST/useEffectST.
Global window registries are treated as the dependency surface.
Object.assign and spread are used for immutable state updates.
ClassName prefixes group UI families: strm-*, gen-opt, wizard__*.
Icon and Btn components are used throughout.
Fallback null/empty states are provided when registry data is missing.
Custom events (vi-stream-complete) broadcast stream results to other surfaces.
concerns: Heavy reliance on window globals makes contracts implicit and hard to test or type.
STProduce defines ftarget inside render and conditionally calls window.useFeedback, which may violate hook rules depending on how useFeedback is implemented.
STReview calls window.useArtifacts at render top-level before an early return, which can be unsafe if it is a React hook.
The runner mutates the step object directly: step._gated = produceGated.
STAttune reads window.MS_GENERATE.QUESTIONS without checking whether MS_GENERATE exists.
Several components assume window globals exist and will throw or render nothing if absent.
If steps is empty, step selection logic can produce an undefined step and crash on step.kind.
The briefSig dependency string serializes answers but not their nested structure changes cleanly.
notes: This file is the glue layer that turns a sequence DSL into an interactive, registry-backed, guided workflow surface usable both for onboarding and inside-project assistance.
questions: What is the exact schema of an MS_STREAMS step object and how are streams authored?
Where are MS_STREAMS, MS_LAUNCH, MS_PRODUCE, MS_ARTIFACTS, MS_MEMORY, MS_DECISIONS, MS_CONTEXT, and MS_GENERATE defined and what are their contracts?
What is the shape of site, selection, and tweaks passed into StreamRunner and StreamHost?
Is window.useFeedback a React hook, and if so is the conditional call in STProduce safe?
What does MS_STREAMS.understanding return and how is canProduce computed?
How do MS_PRODUCE.create and MS_ARTIFACTS.create differ?
What is the role of seedPrompt versus site.selection.note?
Are step ids stable across renders, and is mutating step._gated intentional?
What do ViLearned, CandidateCard, FeedbackLayer, and useArtifacts provide?
How does MS_LAUNCH.context resolve launch.ctx and describe()?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a dynamic, guided workflow surface with a single runner for project setup and sub-sequences that composes registries and explains each step's MOVE.
apparent_themes: Pedagogical transparency through explicit MOVE labels
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to collapse all guided, step-by-step workflows into one reusable stream mechanism that can be launched from anywhere, teach the user what each step does, and produce real project artifacts by orchestrating existing global registries.
novelty: The same runner serves both the full project-init SHAPE wizard and smaller in-project sub-sequences, reusing legacy wizard components inside a synthesize step while surrounding them with a generic sequence rail and understanding model.
contribution: It contributes reusable StreamRunner, StreamLauncher, StreamHost, and per-step renderer components that wire a sequence DSL to global registries, enabling guided workflows to be embedded anywhere and ensuring stream-produced artifacts and preferences feed back into Vi memory.
summary_for_embedding: streams.jsx implements a reusable guided-workflow surface that runs MS_STREAMS sequences, renders step-specific bodies, and commits changes through global registries for project setup and in-project sub-sequences.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single, pedagogical, embeddable stream runner that turns any guided sequence into an interactive, context-aware workflow producing real project state.

symbols.does: Renders a gather step that lets the user add source materials to project context.
symbols.description: STGather shows a drop target button and a list of already-added sources. When clicked, it invokes commit.gather(), which adds a new source via MS_CONTEXT.addSource and updates local state.

symbols.does: Renders a preference step that records a user choice in MS_MEMORY.
symbols.description: STAttune looks up a question from window.MS_GENERATE.QUESTIONS by prefKey and renders option buttons. Selecting an option calls MS_MEMORY.setPreference and shows a toast, then optionally notifies the runner via onPick.

symbols.does: Renders a decision step backed by MS_DECISIONS.
symbols.description: STDecide fetches the decision by decId, highlights the currently active option against the tweaks map, and lets the user pick an option. The choice is applied through commit.decide, which calls setTweak and records the answer in run state.

symbols.does: Renders a produce step that generates artifact candidates for a given kind.
symbols.description: STProduce fetches the kind from MS_KINDS, shows a generate button gated by understanding, and displays produced candidates using KindSpecimen. It creates candidates via MS_PRODUCE or MS_ARTIFACTS and attaches a feedback target to each card.

symbols.does: Builds a feedback target object for a generated artifact candidate.
symbols.description: ftarget is a closure inside STProduce that, when window.useFeedback exists, returns spreadable props linking a candidate card to the feedback system with scope, type, label, and context metadata.

symbols.does: Renders a review step that lists produced candidates for approval or rejection.
symbols.description: STReview prefers artifacts produced during the current run, falling back to project-wide artifacts. It renders CandidateCard components when available, otherwise a simple name-only fallback, and relies on commit.review to persist decisions.

symbols.does: Renders a synthesize step that embeds the project-init SHAPE wizard phases.
symbols.description: STSynthesize maps phase values to window.StepDirection, StepRenderer, StepSequence, and StepReady components. In fullscreen mode it pairs the step body with the window.Deductions panel, replicating the old WizardScreen layout inside the stream runner.

symbols.does: Dispatches a step to its appropriate body component based on step.kind.
symbols.description: STStepBody is a switch router that renders STAsk, STGather, STAttune, STDecide, GenerativeOptions, STProduce, STReview, or STSynthesize, passing the runner's state, commit handlers, context, tweaks, and synth callbacks.

symbols.does: Renders an ask step using the shared QuestionBody component.
symbols.description: STAsk normalizes the step's move object so vi, helper, and options are callable, then delegates rendering to window.QuestionBody with the current answers and an onAnswer callback.

symbols.does: Updates the synthesize-specific patch of run state.
symbols.description: synthUpdate is a helper inside StreamRunner that merges a patch into state.synth, used by STSynthesize to let wizard steps mutate the in-progress project brief.

symbols.does: Moves the stream forward to the next visible step.
symbols.description: advance is a state updater inside StreamRunner that increments ord while clamping it to the last visible step index.

symbols.does: Moves the stream back to the previous visible step.
symbols.description: goBack is a state updater inside StreamRunner that decrements ord while clamping it to zero.

symbols.does: Completes the stream run and broadcasts produced artifact ids.
symbols.description: finish collects artifact ids created during the run, dispatches a vi-stream-complete custom event, and invokes onComplete or onExit to close the runner.

symbols.does: Records an answer from an ask step and advances the stream.
symbols.description: onAnswer merges the new answer into state.answers, optionally stores it as a preference in MS_MEMORY, and immediately advances ord to the next step.
